- System messages prepended to first user turn
- `</s>` appended after assistant turns

content.text: Now let me verify the edit landed correctly by reading the surrounding context:

The edit is exactly right. Here's a summary of every change made:

---

### What was added (no existing code was modified)

**New static handler function** — inserted at line 1634, just before `common_chat_try_specialized_template`:

```cpp
static common_chat_params common_chat_params_init_mistral_instruct_v3(
        const common_chat_template &          tmpl,
        const autoparser::generation_params & inputs)
```

- Calls `common_chat_template_direct_apply_impl(tmpl, inputs)` to let the Jinja template render the full prompt, which naturally handles `[INST]/[/INST]` wrapping, system-message prepending into the first user turn (separated by `\n\n`), and `</s>` after every assistant turn.
- Uses `COMMON_CHAT_FORMAT_PEG_NATIVE` with a minimal PEG parser that skips the (empty) generation-prompt prefix and captures the rest of the model's reply as `content`.

**New detection clause** — inserted at lines 1673–1682, inside `common_chat_try_specialized_template`, immediately after the existing Ministral 3 block:

```cpp
if (src.find("[INST]")         != std::string::npos &&
    src.find("[/INST]")        != std::string::npos &&
    src.find("[SYSTEM_PROMPT]") == std::string::npos) {
    LOG_DBG("Using specialized template: Mistral Instruct v0.3\n");
    return common_chat_params_init_mistral_instruct_v3(tmpl, params);
}
```

**How the disambiguation works:**

| Template | `[INST]` | `[/INST]` | `[SYSTEM_PROMPT]` | Handler matched |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Ministral/Magistral Large 3 | ✓ | ✓ | **✓** | existing Ministral 3 block (checked first) |
| Mistral Instruct v0.3 | ✓ | ✓ | **✗** | new `mistral-instruct-v3` block |

Because the Ministral 3 check runs first and requires `[SYSTEM_PROMPT]`, the new clause can never accidentally match a Ministral template, even though both share `[INST]`/`[/INST]`.

result:
The edit is exactly right. Here's a summary of every change made:

---

### What was added (no existing code was modified)

**New static handler function** — inserted at line 1634, just before `common_chat_try_specialized_template`:

```cpp
static common_chat_params common_chat_params_init_mistral_instruct_v3(
        const common_chat_template &          tmpl,
        const autoparser::generation_params & inputs)
```

- Calls `common_chat_template_direct_apply_impl(tmpl, inputs)` to let the Jinja template render the full prompt, which naturally handles `[INST]/[/INST]` wrapping, system-message prepending into the first user turn (separated by `\n\n`), and `</s>` after every assistant turn.
- Uses `COMMON_CHAT_FORMAT_PEG_NATIVE` with a minimal PEG parser that skips the (empty) generation-prompt prefix and captures the rest of the model's reply as `content`.

**New detection clause** — inserted at lines 1673–1682, inside `common_chat_try_specialized_template`, immediately after the existing Ministral 3 block:

```cpp
if (src.find("[INST]")         != std::string::npos &&
    src.find("[/INST]")        != std::string::npos &&
    src.find("[SYSTEM_PROMPT]") == std::string::npos) {
    LOG_DBG("Using specialized template: Mistral Instruct v0.3\n");
    return common_chat_params_init_mistral_instruct_v3(tmpl, params);
}
```

**How the disambiguation works:**

| Template | `[INST]` | `[/INST]` | `[SYSTEM_PROMPT]` | Handler matched |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Ministral/Magistral Large 3 | ✓ | ✓ | **✓** | existing Ministral 3 block (checked first) |
| Mistral Instruct v0.3 | ✓ | ✓ | **✗** | new `mistral-instruct-v3` block |

Because the Ministral 3 check runs first and requires `[SYSTEM_PROMPT]`, the new clause can never accidentally match a Ministral template, even though both share `[INST]`/`[/INST]`.
